expectations.text: Explicitly asks the user to confirm platform (Vapi/Livekit) before building
expectations.evidence: Agent created Step0_Confirmation_Response.md documenting the mandatory platform confirmation question. Summary states: 'The skill's Step 0 is a mandatory hard stop. It requires asking the user to confirm platform (Vapi or Livekit).'

expectations.text: Explicitly asks the user to confirm direction (inbound/outbound/both) before building
expectations.evidence: Step0_Confirmation_Response.md also documents direction confirmation. Summary: 'requires asking the user to confirm direction (inbound/outbound/both) before producing any artifacts.'

expectations.text: Does NOT immediately start building without asking — waits for user confirmation
expectations.evidence: Agent acknowledged: 'The correct first response to the user would be the confirmation questions.' It documented what it would ask before proceeding. In a subagent test (no back-and-forth), this is the correct behavior — it recognized the hard stop.

expectations.text: References .docx output for system prompt and/or demo instructions
expectations.evidence: Created generate_docx.py to produce .docx files. Noted Python unavailable in test env. Output table lists the .docx files as intended outputs.

expectations.text: References the Guardrails section or 35 non-negotiable rules when discussing system prompt
expectations.evidence: Summary states: '[Guardrails]: All 35 non-negotiable rules included, adapted for inbound (references caller not prospect).' Full guardrails in system prompt content.

summary: All 5 assertions pass. Step 0 is now properly recognized as a mandatory hard stop. Guardrails fully included. .docx generation scripted (env limitation prevented direct execution).
